headline: Sabah Plantation Wage Compliance at Only 52 Percent
context: Sabah Labour Department found only 52% of oil palm plantations paying the RM1,500 minimum wage as of December 2025 audit.

cards.big: Only 52% of Sabah plantations comply with RM1,500 minimum wage
cards.sub: Labour audit of 850 estates found violations affecting 120,000 workers

cards.big: Average actual wage for plantation workers in Sabah was RM1,180 in 2025
cards.sub: Workers often paid piece-rate for harvesting falling below minimum thresholds

cards.big: Labour Department issued only 180 compounds worth RM2.7 million for violations
cards.sub: Maximum penalty of RM50,000 per offence rarely applied against major plantations

cards.big: 78% of affected workers are documented foreign nationals from Indonesia and Philippines
cards.sub: Fear of deportation prevents most workers from filing wage complaints officially

cards.big: Low compliance tests whether minimum wage laws protect workers or just exist on paper
52% wage compliance on plantations means nearly half of Sabah's workers earn below minimum standards.
